diagnoses.name: Hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 5 CKD
diagnoses.evidence_span: hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 5 CKD

diagnoses.name: End stage renal disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: end stage renal disease requiring hemodialysis

diagnoses.name: Alcoholic cirrhosis of the liver without ascites
diagnoses.evidence_span: alcoholic cirrhosis of the liver and concurrent alcoholic hepatitis, both currently without ascites

diagnoses.name: Alcoholic hepatitis without ascites
diagnoses.evidence_span: alcoholic cirrhosis of the liver and concurrent alcoholic hepatitis, both currently without ascites

diagnoses.name: Hepatorenal syndrome
diagnoses.evidence_span: the development of hepatorenal syndrome, which contributed substantially to his acute clinical deterioration

diagnoses.name: Hepatic encephalopathy
diagnoses.evidence_span: Ammonia level was 128 μmol/L, supporting hepatic encephalopathy. Lactulose and rifaxomin were initiated for presumed hepatic encephalopathy

diagnoses.name: Anemia of chronic kidney disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: Anemia secondary to chronic kidney disease. Hemoglobin was 7.8 g/dL, consistent with anemia of chronic kidney disease

procedures.name: Hemodialysis
procedures.evidence_span: end stage renal disease requiring hemodialysis. The patient received emergent hemodialysis on hospital day one

procedures.name: Paracentesis
procedures.evidence_span: ascitic fluid analysis (paracentesis deferred given absence of clinical ascites)

medications.name: Lactulose
medications.evidence_span: Lactulose and rifaxomin were initiated for presumed hepatic encephalopathy

medications.name: Rifaxomicin
medications.evidence_span: Lactulose and rifaxomin were initiated for presumed hepatic encephalopathy

chief_complaint: Altered mental status and acute decompensation
